name: warm-day-setback-heat-mode
description: Warm day + thermostat in heat mode: setback_heat applied instead of HVAC off (Issue #96)
notes: Issue #96: On a warm day the classifier sets hvac_mode='off', but when the thermostat
is currently running in heat mode the correct behavior is to apply the setback_heat
temperature (60F) rather than issuing a full HVAC-off command.
This prevents the thermostat from going cold on a warm morning where heating may still
be needed briefly before outdoor temps rise.
The indoor temp (72F) is above comfort_heat (70F) so the warm-day comfort-gap guard
does NOT fire — the system should proceed directly to setback_heat.
Expected outcome at classification time: setback_applied with target_temp=60.0.
HVAC mode must remain 'heat' — set_hvac_mode('off') must NOT be called.
Simulator support: the simulator _handle_classification() currently sets hvac_mode=off
unconditionally when c.hvac_mode='off'. Issue #96 adds a thermostat-mode-aware branch
that checks the current thermostat hvac_mode and applies setback instead of shutoff.
These assertions are marked simulator_support=false until that branch is implemented.

events.note: Morning temps: indoor 72F (above comfort_heat 70F), outdoor 58F on a warm day
Classifier issues warm-day hvac_mode=off. Thermostat is in heat mode. Expected: setback_heat (60F) applied, no mode change.

assertions.reason: warm day + thermostat in heat mode — setback_heat (60F) applied instead of HVAC off
